name: Brown Rice Casserole
recipeIngredient: Half block of tofu (cut into 1/2-inch cubes)
2 medium zucchini (or other summer squash, finely chopped)
6 ounces mushrooms (wiped clean and finely chopped if small)
1 cup mushroom broth (or vegetable stock or water)
6 ounces grated cheese (Jack, Muenster, Cheddar, or Gouda)
Fresh herbs for garnish (parsley or cilantro)
recipeInstructions:
This dish was one of the most popular at Greens Restaurant in San Francisco.
1. Cook the rice.
2. Prepare the vegetables by chopping them into pieces about 1/2-inch square.
3. Quarter mushrooms if they are small and cut into sixths or eighths if large.
4. Heat olive oil and butter in a pan, then fry onion over medium heat until lightly browned (about 5 minutes).
5. Add garlic, nutritional yeast (if using), cumin, and salt; stir until blended and cook for 1 minute.
6. Add carrots, celery, green pepper, and 1/2 cup of liquid; cover pan and braise vegetables until they begin to soften (about 5 minutes).
7. Add zucchini and mushrooms; cook for 7-10 minutes. The vegetables should be nearly but not completely cooked. If the pan gets dry, add a little more liquid.
8. Combine vegetables with rice and cheese in a lightly oiled casserole dish. Season with salt and plenty of freshly ground black pepper.
9. Mix in tofu gently; cover with foil and bake at 350°F for half an hour.
10. Remove foil and bake for another 15 minutes.
11. Garnish with fresh herbs.
